verses.text: As Solomon ends his prayer, fire falls from heaven and consumes the offering, and the glory of the LORD fills the house so the priests cannot enter; the people bow, crying, 'for he is good; his mercy endureth for ever.' God answers prayer with fire and presence. Heartfelt worship draws down heaven's reply.

As Solomon ends his prayer, fire falls from heaven and consumes the offering, and the glory of the LORD fills the house so the priests cannot enter; the people bow, crying, 'for he is good; his mercy endureth for ever.' God answers prayer with fire and presence. Heartfelt worship draws down heaven's reply.

As Solomon ends his prayer, fire falls from heaven and consumes the offering, and the glory of the LORD fills the house so the priests cannot enter; the people bow, crying, 'for he is good; his mercy endureth for ever.' God answers prayer with fire and presence. Heartfelt worship draws down heaven's reply.

King and people offer sacrifices beyond number and dedicate the house, the courts filled because the altar could not hold all the offerings. Their devotion overflows the appointed place. When the heart is full of God, it gives more lavishly than rule requires.

They keep the feast seven days, then Solomon sends the people home 'glad and merry in heart' for all the goodness God has shown. True worship ends in joy. Those who have met with God go away rejoicing in His kindness.

They keep the feast seven days, then Solomon sends the people home 'glad and merry in heart' for all the goodness God has shown. True worship ends in joy. Those who have met with God go away rejoicing in His kindness.

They keep the feast seven days, then Solomon sends the people home 'glad and merry in heart' for all the goodness God has shown. True worship ends in joy. Those who have met with God go away rejoicing in His kindness.

When all is finished, the LORD appears to Solomon by night: 'I have heard thy prayer, and have chosen this place for an house of sacrifice.' God seals the dedication with His own word. He hears, and He tells His servant that he is heard.

When all is finished, the LORD appears to Solomon by night: 'I have heard thy prayer, and have chosen this place for an house of sacrifice.' God seals the dedication with His own word. He hears, and He tells His servant that he is heard.

God promises that if His people, called by His name, humble themselves, pray, seek His face, and turn from their wicked ways, He will hear, forgive, and heal their land — His eyes and heart there always. Revival begins not with God's reluctance but with His people's repentance.

God promises that if Solomon walks as David did, keeping His statutes, his throne will be established. The covenant runs on through obedience. God's promises are sure, but they are kept along the path of a faithful walk.

God promises that if Solomon walks as David did, keeping His statutes, his throne will be established. The covenant runs on through obedience. God's promises are sure, but they are kept along the path of a faithful walk.

Yet if they turn to serve other gods, God warns He will pluck them from the land and make this glorious house an astonishment, a byword among the nations. Privilege abused becomes judgment. The higher the light, the heavier the fall when it is forsaken.
